data.title: GM is being probed by the NHTSA for a recall remedy that may not have worked
data.description:
The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration opened a review of General Motors (GM) vehicles equipped with L87 6.2L V8 engines that were supposed to be fixed under a prior recall action.

The NHTSA's Office of Defects Investigation received 36 owner responses alleging engine failure in vehicles subject to a previous recall action. The original recall involved 597,630 vehicles from the 2021-2024 model years. The recall was due to connecting rod or crankshaft components that may have manufacturing defects that led to engine damage or failure in some cases. As part of the remedy, dealers were supposed to inspect the engine and make repairs or replacements if needed. After passing inspection, vehicles were switched to a higher viscosity oil.

"Recall 25V-274 addresses vehicles equipped with an L87 6.2L V8 gas engine that experienced engine damage or failure. Recall 25V-274’s remedy includes an engine inspection.  Based on the results of the inspection, the vehicle will receive an oil change to a higher viscosity oil or an engine replacement.  The complaints received allege failures of both remedies."

Shares of General Motors (GM) were down 1.0% in premarket trading.

data.title: D.R. Horton Q1 earnings beat as net orders increase; guidance reaffirmed
data.description:
D.R. Horton (DHI) delivered far better-than-expected fiscal Q1 earnings as the number of homes it sold topped the consensus and net orders increased from a year ago. The homebuilder reiterated its prior FY2026 guidance, including revenue of $33.5B-$35.0B ($34.3B midpoint) vs. $34.3B consensus.

However, the company expects sales incentives to remain elevated as affordability issues continue to weigh on demand.

"During the first quarter, our tenured operators continued to execute with discipline while driving a 3% increase in net sales orders," said Executive Chairman David Auld. "Affordability constraints and cautious consumer sentiment continue to impact new home demand."

Fiscal Q1 EPS of $2.03, blowing past the average analyst estimate of $1.02, declined from $2.61 in the year-ago quarter.

Q1 revenue of $6.89B, topping the $6.65B consensus, dropped 9.5% from $7.61B in Q1 2025.

Net sales orders of 18,300 increased 2.6% from 17,837 in the year-ago period, while order value of $6.66B edged up from $6.65B.

The company closed on the sale of 17,818 homes, valued at $6.51B, during the quarter, exceeding the Visible Alpha consensus of 17,275, even though the number declined from 19,059, valued at $7.15B, in Q1 2025.

Sales order backlog of 11,376 units, valued at $4.31B, rose from 11,003 units valued at $4.30B.

Homebuilding gross margin was 23.8% vs. 24.7% in the prior quarter and 26.4% in the year-ago quarter.

Cancellation rate of 18% was consistent with the prior-year quarter.

Selling, general, and administrative expense of $865.1M declined from $878.1M in Q1 2025.

D.R. Horton (DHI) stock edged down 0.1% in Tuesday premarket trading.

data.title: Gilat Satellite Networks secures $11M SkyEdge platform order
data.description:
• Gilat Satellite Networks (GILT) announced on Tuesday that its commercial division received a contract for approximately $11M for its SkyEdge platform from a satellite operator in the Asia Pacific region.
  • The platform will be deployed to deliver services over Very High Throughput Satellites (VHTS), supporting multiple applications, the company said. 
  • Deliveries are expected over the next 12 months, it added.
  • GILT -1.73% premarket to $17.55.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PowerBank unveils 5 MW solar-plus-storage project
data.description:
• PowerBank Corporation (SUUN) has executed a lease agreement on a 5 MW AC hybrid solar-plus battery energy storage project known as the NY-Sapbush project in New York, it said on Tuesday.
  • The project is expected to be eligible for incentives under the New York State Energy Research and Development Authority NY-Sun and Retail Energy Storage Incentive Programs.
  • PowerBank Corporation (SUUN) is down 4.4% premarket.

data.title: Willis Lease Finance declares $0.40 dividend
data.description:
• Willis Lease Finance (WLFC) declared $0.40/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.87%
  • Payable Feb. 20; for shareholders of record Feb. 11; ex-div Feb. 11.
  • See WLFC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Entegris shares dip as CFO steps down, guidance reaffirmed
data.description:
• Entegris (ENTG) Tuesday said that Linda LaGorga will step down as chief financial officer, by agreement, effective February 28, and reaffirmed its outlook for the fourth quarter.
  • Effective March 1, 2026, Mike Sauer, Entegris’ VP, controller, and chief accounting officer, will assume the role of interim CFO, in addition to maintaining the responsibilities of his current role. 
  • Entegris has initiated a comprehensive search process for a permanent CFO with the assistance of a leading executive search firm.
  • Entegris (ENTG) shares are down ~3% premarket.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: U.S. Bancorp beats Q4 on strong NII, fee growth
Soluna taps Michael Picchi as CFO
data.description:
• Soluna Holdings (SLNH) has appointed Michael Picchi as chief financial officer and treasurer, effective April 1, 2026.
  • Picchi most recently served as chief financial officer and chief accounting officer at data center developer TECFusions. He will lead Soluna’s finance organization and support the company’s capital strategy as it scales its behind-the-meter, renewable-powered data center model and expands into Al.
  • David Michaels, a member of Soluna’s board of directors, has served as interim chief financial officer and treasurer since August 2025. Michaels will continue in his role as chief financial officer through March 31, 2026, and remain on Soluna’s board.
  • SLNH --4.2% premarket to $1.599
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eve Air Mobility gets $150M debt for aircraft development
data.description:
• Eve Air Mobility (EVEX) secured $150M in debt financing to boost its electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft development.
  • This funding comes from a syndicate including Itaú, Banco do Brasil, Citibank, and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group as a five-year loan.
  • The proceeds will support Eve's research and development, including the integration of its eVTOL aircraft into a comprehensive urban air mobility ecosystem. This funding accelerates technological progress and strengthens partnerships with infrastructure providers and regulatory bodies. 
  • This brings Eve's total funding to $1.2B and aims for commercialization through 2028.
  • The company recently completed the first flight of its full-scale engineering prototype at Embraer's test facility in Brazil.

data.title: Digital Currency X Technology announces 12 for 1 share consolidation
